guardrails.title: Generic Font Prohibition
guardrails.body: Do not use generic font names (e.g. Arial, Times New Roman, Helvetica) directly. Define project fonts as CSS custom properties or design tokens and reference those instead.

guardrails.title: Color System Required
guardrails.body: All colors shall be defined as CSS custom properties (e.g. `--color-primary`). Hard-coded hex/rgb values in component styles are prohibited. Use the project's color system.

guardrails.title: Animation Method Constraint
guardrails.body: Prefer CSS animations and transitions over JavaScript-driven animations. Use JavaScript animation only when CSS cannot achieve the required effect (e.g. scroll-linked, physics-based).

guardrails.title: No Inline Styles in Components
guardrails.body: Inline `style` attributes in component templates are prohibited. Use CSS classes, CSS modules, or styled-components instead.

guardrails.title: Responsive Breakpoints Must Use Design Tokens
guardrails.body: Media query breakpoints shall reference design tokens or shared variables. Hard-coded pixel values in individual components are prohibited.

guardrails.title: Accessibility Minimum for Interactive Elements
guardrails.body: All interactive elements (buttons, links, form controls) shall have accessible labels, sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA), and keyboard operability.

guardrails.title: Image Format and Optimization Required
guardrails.body: Images shall use modern formats (WebP, AVIF) with appropriate fallbacks. Unoptimized images (BMP, uncompressed PNG/TIFF) are prohibited in production builds.
